steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to specify the order in which government entities received authority to regulate US airline safety. The correct choice must present the chronological sequence of transfers explicitly.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "The authority… transferred from the US Commerce Department to the CAA in 1938, then from the CAA to the FAA in 1958.": Gives the full two-step sequence with dates, directly answering the order question. ✓
  • Choice B — "…at which point authority was transferred to the US Commerce Department by Elwood R. Quesada.": Factually wrong — authority went from the CAA to the FAA in 1958, not back to the Commerce Department. ✗
  • Choice C — "The FAA, CAA, and the US Commerce Department all had the authority… at different times.": True but vague — gives no chronological order. ✗
  • Choice D — "The FAA has regulated airline safety since it was established by the US Congress in 1958.": Mentions only the final transfer and omits the earlier sequence. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
